pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Cabrera had raw K stuff and plus-money dislocation at 6+, but the play carried a major Coors Field volatility downgrade. The correct construction was small-only with no 7+ ladder.

what_worked: The model correctly identified this as a small-only volatility stab rather than a core position.
The no 7+ ladder rule was correct; Cabrera finished with 5 strikeouts.
Secondary pitches did generate strikeout conversion: curveball and slider produced all 5 strikeouts.
what_failed: The 6+ threshold required one more strikeout, but overall CSW collapsed to 19%, well below the 30% green zone.
Fastball and sinker velocity were elevated, but neither pitch converted into strikeouts.
Cabrera allowed 9 hard-hit balls on 15 BBE, confirming the Coors/contact danger profile.
Changeup did not function as a reliable whiff engine: only 1 whiff on 8 swings and 13% CSW.
risk_notes: This loss was not a pure leash failure. Cabrera had 83 pitches, 5.1 IP, and 22 batters faced, which was enough runway to contend for 6 Ks.
The failure mode was pitch-quality conversion: velocity was strong, but CSW and fastball/sinker whiff conversion were too low.
Coors/high-total environments should require a higher CSW projection or deeper discount before attacking 6+ thresholds.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Add COORS_PLUS_MONEY_VOLATILITY_CAP: even when plus-money edge exists, cap stake at micro/small and require either projected CSW >= 30% or live confirmation before attacking 6+ in Coors/high-total environments. Prefer live over pregame when primary fastball/sinker whiff conversion is uncertain.

notes: Cabrera landed 5 Ks and missed the 6+ ticket by one. The postmortem confirms the original risk label: raw stuff was present, but Coors contact pressure and poor overall CSW prevented threshold conversion.
